signals.headline: South Korea's headline CPI inflation held steady at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's headline CPI inflation held steady at 2.0% YoY in February 2026, with core inflation rising to 2.3% amid increasing upside risks from oil prices and KRW weakness | NO | HIGH confidence**[1][2]

ING analysts report no new inflation data release today, only analysis of February's steady 2.0% headline CPI (down from prior expectations in some metrics) and rising core to 2.3%, prompting
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.raw:
Lebanon war ongoing** with IDF intensifying operations (new plans approved, 1,700+ Hezbollah killed), no ceasefire decision despite US-pushed Israel-Lebanon leader talks (first in 34 years).[1][4]  
House rejected war powers resolution limiting Trump (213-214 vote, April 17).[3]  
Trump's April 18 briefing mentioned (in progress per liveblog), but no details confirm resolution.[7] **LOW confidence

signals.raw: Kerala Legislative Assembly elections** (held April 9, 2026) feature ongoing pre/post-poll controversies, including a row over an alleged **communal campaign** by LDF against **IUML** woman candidate Fathima Thahliya in Perambra, with UDF/Congress criticizing LDF and no Election Commission action confirmed yet[1].
